reviews.review_msg: Course needs focus, due to a personal story style that makes learning useful content challenging. Vague course outline makes writing the term papers difficult. Not the best or a useful communication course for anything productive.

Professor Gruneau is smart, nice, interesting and an overall great professor. His lectures are like stories but if you are paying attention full of important information regarding the course subject. Rick is approachable and extremely knowledgeable. I recommend him as an instructor whatever the course may be. Super solid guy.

Rick has an uncanny ability to explain difficult concepts and make you understand why they are important to communications. He has a breadth of knowledge that can be intimidating, but he's actually very approachable. My favorite prof in communications.
Rick is excellent. Lectures are extremely interesting and he is always ready for extra help. I took the course in the fall and have never received so much help from a Prof. Great Job Rick :)

Rick is excellent. Lectures are extremely interesting and he is always ready for extra help. I took the course in the fall and have never received so much help from a Prof. Great Job Rick :) Thanks a ton.
Rick will give you extra help when you go to his office hour. He would like to give you many interesting examples to help you understanding concepts. He is a very nice professor!

Rick does a great job at explaining very complex ideas. If you attend class every week, you will do well on the papers. The course covers crucial concepts that will bring value to the knowledge of someone majoring in communications.

If you are looking for 'structured learning' as a poster below noted, stay away from anything Gruneau teaches!! What you get instead is passion, incredible insight and flashes of real brilliance. Fair trade I'd say. This class inspired me to apply to grad school.

Can only speak about my own experience but I found Gruneau to be extremely helpful. He was always available for discussion and he went out of his way to write several reference letters for me. As for course content I thought 410 was the most comprehensive and challenging course I took in Communications.

Could be an interesting class but the failure to follow the course outline hinders structured learning.  Personal storytelling has little to do with the content but seems more about him appearing "cool".  He skips or is never available during his office hours and impossible to reach outside of  class.  Becomes annoyed when students ask questions.

At his best gruneau is surely one of the best lecturers at SFU. He has an aptitude for synthesizing ideas and theories, and for explaining difficult concepts, that is unique. He can wander a bit sometimes but  most often his apparent  digressions reveal themselves to serve the point he is trying to make. Amazing prof and one of a kind.
I like the way he teaches by using stories to clarify difficult concepts and theories. He's also very approachable and he'll give you lots of help if you ask. Good prof.

Narrative style of teaching meanders all over the place, based upon personal stories.  Would be better to stick to teaching from texts (there is a course text).  Impossible to contact outside of class as for many students experience.  Please don't bring up your water skiing accident, it has nothing to do with the course, we do not care - focus plz.

I was nervous about taking 410 because I heard it was hard and a lot of work. But Gruneau has a great way of explaining things and he made the class enjoyable. In the end I thought it was a great course.

Withing 3 weeks of sitting in 410 you just get the impression you are in a whole other league from the rest of the courses in communications. This was the best course in 5 years at SFU and Gruneau weaves a theoretical story in the class that is nothing short of brilliant.

Thoroughly enjoyed his lectures, though they were long and sometimes rambling.  The material is dense and very theoretical, but his lectures are entertaining even when the material isn't.  Fair marker, though the papers were all long, expect to write no less than 10 pages per paper for him, no shorter papers at all.
Amazing lecturer, but the material is really difficult. You have to be really into history and theory to like his classes. But for theory heads Gruneau is the Man!

He is a good at lecturing but goes off topic and its confusing. The subjects in this class is BORING. You will most likely fall asleep or get lost and if you look around half the class will be lost too.
loved his lectures. he is a great storyteller  and great educator. i cant get enough of his lectures.

Gruneau is a great guy and an entertaining and hilarious storyteller.  I love the way he teaches, it is so refreshing to just listen to a prof rather than try to listen and read a bunch of notes at the same time.  He is a very GOOD marker, which means that he can tell how much effort you actually put into something.
Great course if you want to listen to rambling life stories on a weekly basis! Although I did not mind coming to class, I did not enjoy his unconventional teaching style.

Excellent lecturer. I look forward to going to lec every week. Difficult to take notes because his teaching style is very story-orientated. Its a pleasure to listen to him talk about what he is obviously passionate about.

Rick is an awesome prof when it comes be being passionate about hat he's teaching about. He does tend to stick with one topic forever. If you are taking this class to talk sports, DON'T its a painful class for people who want to talk current sports. But its cool if you can put that aside and you learn alot sports media history and society

Very busy and hard to get hold of sometimes, and he jumps around a lot in lectures. But, he teaches with a passion that is just amazing. Brilliant, and often hilarious. If there is more entertaining lecturer at SFU I don't know who it is.
rad.  very helpful, etnertaining and smart. tough marker, but reasonably so.

Excellent prof! Here's a prof who doesn't use those flashy PowerPoint lecture notes, just plain old chalk and board, a cup of coffee, and a REAL PASSION FOR TEACHING! He has this amazing ability to explain very complex and nuanced concepts in an entertaining way. Highly recommended!
Communications resident genius. Best prof I've ever had...caring, and a great guy. Great lecturing style. Unlike any other prof at the university. don't miss taking a class with him.

Very interesting guy...you will not be bored in his class...but you will be challenged...and he's a really funny person too...usually schedules his classes friday mornings though :( if you ask for help, he'll give it to you
His lectures are enjoyable, but his essay markings are not!
My second course with him. Best prof I've had in 4 years at SFU. Brilliant, really funny at times and a fantastic lecturer.

THE best lecturer, even if he does skip around a bit much...lots of good anecdotes, very conversational, easy to listen to...seems gruff, but is willing to help you out if you do your background work. Take anything with him, especially if you like sports.
He has a very good lecturing style.  He's funny and informative at the same time.  If you want to enjoy your lectures, take one of his classes.
Evil, but knowledgeable. Doesn't care at all about students, but possibly cares about Marx. Can talk for hours, but hates to listen. Brilliant, but mostly to himself.
Great professor...makes school interesting...
Unreal lecturer, but so busy he can be hard to get a hold of outside of class. Great attitude and really knows his stuff.
A great lecturer. Courses are challenging but enlightening.
great prof who brought the course topics to life through is stories
Entertaining, passionate, and really knowledgeable.
very high on himself, tries hard to make the course too difficult to get an A
His courses are really challenging. But, he's a fantastic lecturer.
Amazing Prof!
